name: Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail
name_original: La Ruta de Los Mineros desde Orgiva — circular por el Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada
description: Tackle the challenging Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail, a 25.8-mile mountain biking route with 5663 feet of elevation gain and panoramic view
region_intro: Mountain biking around Órgiva offers a diverse and dramatic natural landscape in the heart of the Alpujarras region. The area is characterized by the mountainous terrain of the Sierra Nevada and Sierra de Lújar, providing significant inclines and descents. The Guadalfeo River Valley adds varied topography, while gorges and ravines contribute to the challenging and technical riding experience. This region is a hub for mountain biking, featuring a vast network of routes. Best mountain bike trails around Órgiva The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail, a 25.8 miles (41.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and offers panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Lújar — circular desde Órgiva por el barranco de los Castillejos, a difficult 26.3 miles (42.3 km) path. This route involves substantial climbing through varied mountain terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Capileira Village – Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint loop from Órgiva, a 38.9 miles (62.7 km) trail leading through the high Alpujarras landscape, often completed in about 5 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Órgiva is defined by the dramatic inclines of the Sierra Nevada and Sierra de Lújar, the varied topography of the Guadalfeo River Valley, and numerous gorges and ravines. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Órgiva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Órgiva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Órgiva?
A: Mountain biking around Órgiva is known for its challenging terrain. Out of 11 routes, 10 are rated as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills. There is also 1 &lt;strong&gt;moderate&lt;/strong&gt; route available for those with good fitness.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails in Órgiva for less experienced riders?
A: Yes, while most routes are difficult, there is one moderate trail: the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10303635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Órgiva – House of the Witches, Soportújar loop from Órgiva&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers 22.5 km with 703 meters of elevation gain, offering a less strenuous option.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Órgiva?
A: The terrain is diverse and dramatic, characterized by the mountainous landscapes of the Sierra Nevada and Sierra de Lújar. You'll encounter significant inclines and descents, varied topography in the Guadalfeo River Valley, and numerous gorges and ravines, providing a challenging and technical riding experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Órgiva?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022078862/sierra-de-lujar-circular-desde-orgiva-por-el-barranco-de-los-castillejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Lújar — circular desde Órgiva por el barranco de los Castillejos&lt;/a&gt; is a 42.3 km loop with substantial climbing. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31547681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capileira Village – Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint loop from Órgiva&lt;/a&gt;, a strenuous 62.7 km ride through the high Alpujarras.

Q: What are the best times of year to go mountain biking in Órgiva?
A: The region experiences a mild, almost subtropical climate, making it suitable for riding for much of the year. While mountain biking is popular, especially during May and August, the pleasant climate generally allows for good conditions outside of the hottest summer months or potential winter snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or panoramic vistas along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. Many trails offer breathtaking scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185293067/sierra-de-lujar-antennas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is known for its panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31547681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capileira Village – Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint loop from Órgiva&lt;/a&gt; also leads to the Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint, offering stunning high Alpujarras landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Órgiva?
A: The mountain bike routes in Órgiva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders often praise the dramatic inclines, varied topography, and the challenging yet rewarding experience of exploring the Sierra Nevada and Sierra de Lújar.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the Alpujarras region is rich in natural features. You can find several waterfalls nearby, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cahorros de Nigüelas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which maintains flow even in dry periods, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2777071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lanjarón River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These can be explored as detours or part of a longer trip.

Q: What are some of the highest elevation gains on mountain bike trails in Órgiva?
A: The trails around Órgiva feature significant elevation gains due to the mountainous terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10303705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capileira – Capileira Village loop from Órgiva&lt;/a&gt; involves over 2,070 meters of ascent, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31547681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capileira Village – Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint loop from Órgiva&lt;/a&gt; has over 2,020 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find mountain passes or other significant landmarks along the trails?
A: Yes, the region features several mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1014164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haza del Lino Pass&lt;/a&gt; is a notable point, often including a restaurant. Other passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4376058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Pitres&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5506146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountain Road Between Pampaneira and Pitres&lt;/a&gt; offer challenging climbs and scenic views.

Q: How long do the mountain bike trails typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies significantly based on the trail's length and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185293067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail&lt;/a&gt; (41.5 km) typically takes around 4 hours 34 minutes, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31547681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capileira Village – Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint loop from Órgiva&lt;/a&gt; (62.7 km) can take about 5 hours 48 minutes.

The Lújar antennas are located at an altitude of 1,850 meters. The climb on both sides of the path is demanding, but it is worth the effort to reach the top and enjoy 360º views of the Granada mountains.
Natural viewpoint located on top of the Los Majanillos mountain, in the Sierra de Lújar, with views of the Guadalfeo Valley and the Mediterranean coast of Granada.
Orgiva bridge, a point very frequented by cyclists, bikers and weekenders who visit the Alpujarras

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a very demanding and varied surface. You'll encounter sections of rough road, some tarmac that can be in poor condition or even absent, and rough tracks with steep switchbacks. The final 2 kilometers towards the antennas can have irregular or absent asphalt. It's a challenging ride that requires a robust bike.

faqs.question: What type of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the extremely rough and varied road surface, including unpaved and broken sections, a mountain bike or a sturdy gravel bike is highly recommended. A robust road bike might struggle with the challenging conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on the Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking 360° panoramic views, especially from the summit of Sierra Lújar. These vistas include the Alboran Sea, the Sierra de la Contraviesa, and a clear view of the Sierra Nevada to the north. The ascent itself offers continuously changing and interesting scenery due to constant bends and hairpins.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail?
faqs.answer: Key highlights along the route include the challenging climb to the telecommunication towers (Antenas de Lújar) at the summit, which offers unparalleled panoramic views. You'll also pass by Cortijos del Puerto Camacho, enjoy the View from Los Majanillos, and cross the Puente de Órgiva.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered highly challenging, often categorized as "Hors Catégorie" (HC) for cycling, indicating extreme difficulty. It features significant climbs with sections reaching gradients of 10-15%. Very good fitness is required to tackle this demanding route.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for the Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail starts near Órgiva. While specific parking details aren't provided, Órgiva is a common starting point for routes in the area, and you would typically find parking options within or on the outskirts of the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned for dogs on this specific trail, it's a demanding mountain biking route. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is well-conditioned for long, strenuous climbs and rough terrain, and always keep it under control, preferably on a leash, to ensure its safety and that of other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail?
faqs.answer: Considering the significant elevation and exposure, spring and autumn are generally the best times to ride this trail. Summers can be very hot, especially during the strenuous climbs, and winters might bring colder temperatures or even snow at higher elevations. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to mountain bike the Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the Sierra de Lújar Antennas Trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Camino Antenas de Sierra Lújar, Ruta de los Mineros, Camino de Vélez, and Vereda Antenas de Lújar, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a sense of remoteness, especially as you ascend towards the antennas. It's advisable to carry all necessary water and snacks with you, as amenities like cafes or official water sources are unlikely to be found directly on the more challenging sections of the route. Plan to resupply in towns like Órgiva before or after your ride.
